brief:
Bitcoin trades below its 200-day moving average with a death cross confirmed, while Fear and Greed sits at 23—deep capitulation territory. BTC dominance remains elevated at 56.41%, suggesting defensive positioning and flight-to-core assets. The 5-day drawdown of -2.94% is modest in z-score terms (-0.58), indicating recent weakness is not statistical. Cycle phase cannot be determined from available signals, but the structure points toward late-stage bear or early bottoming consolidation.

The critical tension: capitulation sentiment and technical breakdown (MA cross) are present, but positioning data (funding rates, altcoin rotation metrics) are unavailable to confirm whether this is panic capitulation or structural unwind. SOL's -1.36 z-score against BTC suggests risk appetite deterioration. Without derivatives and rotation signals, conviction on a tactical dislocation play is limited.
